opportunity.title: Jeriko – an AI agent that runs directly inside your OS
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: The OS-level integration is interesting because it sidesteps the abstraction layer that most agents sit behind. When an agent hooks into system calls directly, you can skip the ritual of "writing a file to disk" and just ask it to create a file. That means the agent's reasoning loop is one step closer to the actual computational substrate, which changes how you think about sandboxing and permissions. The real question isn't whether the agent is "smart," but whether the OS can safely delegate authority to a reasoning process that isn't running in a container.
actions.reasoning: Jeriko's approach to running AI agents directly within the OS touches on system integration, terminal interfaces, and how agent reasoning interfaces with the underlying system architecture.
